section: BILINGUAL (ENGLISH AND HINDI IN ROMAN CHARACTER)—POETRY.
author: W. F. Johnson, Revd., D.D.
title: Hindi Arrows for the Preacher's Bow. Dharmm Dohawali
gloss: A collection of Hindi couplets for the use of preachers.
notes: 'Hindi Arrows for the Preacher's Bow' — Hindi devotional couplets (in Roman script) for CHRISTIAN preachers, by an American missionary (Rev. W. F. Johnson, D.D.); the missionary appropriation of the Hindi doha form for evangelism, in Roman script — a triple-hybrid artefact (Christian content, Hindi verse-form, Roman script). Reg 6 = very early. Published from Allahabad. On-theme for the observation-operator / form-appropriation.

section: BILINGUAL (ENGLISH IN URDU CHARACTERS AND URDU)—MISCELLANEOUS.
author: Mahbub-i-Alam
title: Zaruri Angrezi Alfaz
gloss: A glossary of important English words and technical terms of every day use.
notes: 'Necessary English Words' — a glossary of everyday English terms transliterated INTO Urdu script, 3rd ed. (Mahbub Alam of Paisa Akhbar again, cf. p34 s20). A precise artefact of lexical colonization: English technical/administrative vocabulary entering vernacular usage via Urdu-script phonetic rendering. Directly indexes language-contact/loanword absorption — the reification of English terms into the lifeworld.

printer_verbatim: The Punjab Educational Printing Works, Lahore
author: Bhanu Dat, Pandit
title: Vyakaran Bhanu
gloss: An elementary Sanskrit grammar in Hindi.
publisher: The Manager, Shri Krishen Book Depot
notes: Elementary Sanskrit grammar taught through Hindi, an established 10th edition — the Sanskrit-revival pedagogy (learning the sacred/classical language via the modern vernacular); the Arya-Samaj/DAV Sanskrit-education program. '(Designed for educational purposes.)'.

title: Arsh Granthawali, Shrimad Bhagvad Gita
gloss: The Arsh Granthawali Series, Volume V, No. 9½, for September and October, 1909. The text of the Gita with Hindi translation and a brief independent commentary by Pandit Raja Ram.
notes: Bhagavad Gita (Sanskrit text + Hindi translation/commentary) as a serial installment of the Arsh Granthawali Series — the Arya-Samaj scripture-publication project (Dayananda's 'Arsh'/'seer-given' canon); a subscription serial issuing the Sanskrit classics in Hindi. verso index: '46.1-4 Arsha Granthavali II'. Bracket note: previous issue at entry No. 13, page 44 of the Catalogue for the quarter ending 31st December 1909. This is the 'December 1909' cross-reference text (back-issues) that superficially looked like a 1909 supplement.

title: Arsh Granthawali, Shrimad Bhagvad Gita, Volume V, No. 11
gloss: Volume V, No. 10½, for October and November, 1909.
notes: Continuation installment of the Gita serial (pp. 113-176), Oct-Nov 1909 number registered Jan 1910. Dash-continuation of s1.

title: Arsh Granthawali, Shrimad Bhagvad Gita, Volume V, No. 12
notes: Final Gita installment (pp. 177-224), December 1909 number. Dash-continuation of s2 — the Gita serial completes Volume V across three Q1-registered numbers.

title: Arsh Granthawali Ken-Upanishad
gloss: The text of the Kena-Upanishad with Hindi translation and an independent Hindi commentary.
notes: Kena-Upanishad (Sanskrit + Hindi commentary) in the same Arsh Granthawali series — the Arya-Samaj systematic issuing of the Upanishads alongside the Gita; the canon-formation of a 'Hindu scripture' set in modern serial form.
